sections.title: Executive Synthesis
sections.content:
- Outcome: Expansion into compliance mid-market and regulated SMB can add $44-61M ARR over 24 months.
- Core segment growth decelerated from 22% to 11% YoY while CAC payback stretched from 14 to 18 months.
- Segment B win-rate is 1.8x Segment C at current positioning and shows 31% lower onboarding failure.
- Primary bottleneck is onboarding throughput, not demand generation; implementation queue is now the critical path.
- Recommendation: sequence Segment B first, complete onboarding redesign in Q3, then open Segment C in Q4.
- Decision required this quarter: approve $6.2M enablement envelope tied to milestone gates.

sources.label: Internal CRM and pipeline analytics, Jan 2026

sections.title: Market And Conversion Signals
sections.content:
- U.S. compliance spend growth: 9.4
- Adjacent segment annual growth: 17.0
- Segment B conversion rate: 28.0
- Segment C conversion rate: 15.6
- Average sales cycle (days): 74
- Sales cycle after onboarding redesign (target): 54
- Segment B economics indicate superior speed-to-revenue under current product-market fit.

sections.title: Unit Economics And Capacity Baseline
sections.content:
| Metric | FY23 | TTM Jun-24 | Target FY26 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Pipeline coverage (x) | 2.4 | 2.1 | 2.8 |
| Net revenue retention (%) | 113 | 118 | 120 |
| CAC payback (months) | 14 | 18 | 13 |
| Gross margin (%) | 74.2 | 76.1 | 78.0 |
| Onboarding cycle time (days) | 68 | 61 | 45 |
- Economics support expansion only if onboarding cycle time is reduced below 50 days.
- Margin headroom exists, but CAC deterioration requires sequencing discipline.

sources.label: Finance and operations baseline model v5

sections.title: SWOT Assessment
sections.content:
- Strength: Enterprise retention remains 118% with low logo churn and stable gross margin mix.
- Weakness: Time-to-value averages 61 days, 2.2x peer benchmark, driven by integration handoffs.
- Opportunity: Mid-market compliance vertical is growing 17% annually with low incumbent depth.
- Threat: Competitors bundle implementation services at discounted rates to compress sales cycles.
- Strategic implication: win the next phase through onboarding speed and implementation certainty, not price discounting.

sections.title: Option Tradeoff
sections.content:
- Option A (Broad expansion): highest ARR upside but elevated execution risk and diluted focus.
- Option B (Phased expansion): balanced ARR upside with strongest control on payback and delivery risk.
- Option C (Core optimization): lowest risk but insufficient growth against board plan.
- Criteria weighting: growth potential (35), execution risk (30), payback speed (20), strategic fit (15).
- Weighted scores: Option A 72, Option B 84, Option C 63.

sections.title: Decision Gates And Milestones
sections.content:
| Milestone gate | Timing | KPI threshold | Owner |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Segment B pilot readiness | Month 2 | Onboarding SLA < 55 days | COO |
| Segment B launch gate | Month 4 | Conversion >= 24% | CRO |
| Segment C readiness gate | Month 7 | CAC payback <= 15 months | CFO |
| Segment C launch decision | Month 9 | NRR >= 117% and queue < 2 weeks | CEO |
- Gate-based sequencing prevents premature expansion and protects payback profile.

sections.title: Implementation Timeline
sections.content:
- Month 0-2: finalize segment playbook and deploy dedicated implementation pod.
- Month 2-4: redesign onboarding workflow and automate top 5 integration handoffs.
- Month 4-6: launch Segment B and run weekly conversion/cycle-time governance.
- Month 6-8: complete post-launch stabilization and uplift playbook.
- Month 8-9: evaluate Segment C readiness against gate thresholds.

sections.title: Top Risks And Mitigations
sections.content:
- Risk: onboarding queue overload could delay booked ARR realization by one quarter.
- Risk: segment mix drift may compress gross margin if discounting discipline weakens.
- Risk: implementation staffing gaps could increase delivery failure rate above tolerance.
- Mitigation: enforce queue threshold triggers with contingency staffing pool.
- Mitigation: implement pricing guardrails and executive discount approvals.
- Mitigation: establish weekly delivery QA and integration defect triage cadence.
